data.title: Shareholders Will Be Pleased With The Quality of DUAL's (KRX:016740) Earnings
data.description:

How Do Unusual Items Influence Profit?

To properly understand DUAL's profit results, we need to consider the ₩8.2b expense attributed to unusual items. While deductions due to unusual items are disappointing in the first instance, there is a silver lining. We looked at thousands of listed companies and found that unusual items are very often one-off in nature. And that's hardly a surprise given these line items are considered unusual. If DUAL doesn't see those unusual expenses repeat, then all else being equal we'd expect its profit to increase over the coming year.

Our Take On DUAL's Profit Performance

Because unusual items detracted from DUAL's earnings over the last year, you could argue that we can expect an improved result in the current quarter. data.title: OPTRONTEC Inc. (KOSDAQ:082210) May Have Run Too Fast Too Soon With Recent 26% Price Plummet
data.description:
OPTRONTEC Inc. (KOSDAQ:082210) shareholders won't be pleased to see that the share price has had a very rough month, dropping 26% and undoing the prior period's positive performance. Instead of being rewarded, shareholders who have already held through the last twelve months are now sitting on a 12% share price drop.

Even after such a large drop in price, there still wouldn't be many who think OPTRONTEC's price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 0.3x is worth a mention when the median P/S in Korea's Electronic industry is similar at about 0.7x. Although, it's not wise to simply ignore the P/S without explanation as investors may be disregarding a distinct opportunity or a costly mistake.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

View our latest analysis for OPTRONTEC

Advertisement

What Does OPTRONTEC's Recent Performance Look Like?

As an illustration, revenue has deteriorated at OPTRONTEC over the last year, which is not ideal at all. It might be that many expect the company to put the disappointing revenue performance behind them over the coming period, which has kept the P/S from falling. If not, then existing shareholders may be a little nervous about the viability of the share price.

Want the full picture on earnings, revenue and cash flow for the company? Then our free report on OPTRONTEC will help you shine a light on its historical performance.

How Is OPTRONTEC's Revenue Growth Trending?

The only time you'd be comfortable seeing a P/S like OPTRONTEC's is when the company's growth is tracking the industry closely.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered a frustrating 21% decrease to the company's top line. As a result, revenue from three years ago have also fallen 6.1% overall. So unfortunately, we have to acknowledge that the company has not done a great job of growing revenue over that time.

Weighing that medium-term revenue trajectory against the broader industry's one-year forecast for expansion of 19% shows it's an unpleasant look.

In light of this, it's somewhat alarming that OPTRONTEC's P/S sits in line with the majority of other companies. Apparently many investors in the company are way less bearish than recent times would indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock right now. There's a good chance existing shareholders are setting themselves up for future disappointment if the P/S falls to levels more in line with the recent negative growth rates.

The Key Takeaway

OPTRONTEC's plummeting stock price has brought its P/S back to a similar region as the rest of the industry. Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-sales ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

The fact that OPTRONTEC currently trades at a P/S on par with the rest of the industry is surprising to us since its recent revenues have been in decline over the medium-term, all while the industry is set to grow. Even though it matches the industry, we're uncomfortable with the current P/S ratio, as this dismal revenue performance is unlikely to support a more positive sentiment for long. Unless the recent medium-term conditions improve markedly, investors will have a hard time accepting the share price as fair value.

You should always think about risks. Case in point, we've spotted 3 warning signs for OPTRONTEC you should be aware of, and 2 of them can't be ignored.

If these risks are making you reconsider your opinion on OPTRONTEC, explore our interactive list of high quality stocks to get an idea of what else is out there.

data.title: Inbound Platform's (TSE:5587) Earnings May Just Be The Starting Point
data.description:
Even though Inbound Platform Corp. (TSE:5587 ) posted strong earnings, investors appeared to be underwhelmed. Our analysis says that investors should be optimistic, as the strong profit is built on solid foundations.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

Zooming In On Inbound Platform's Earnings

One key financial ratio used to measure how well a company converts its profit to free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio. To get the accrual ratio we first subtract FCF from profit for a period, and then divide that number by the average operating assets for the period. The ratio shows us how much a company's profit exceeds its FCF.

As a result, a negative accrual ratio is a positive for the company, and a positive accrual ratio is a negative. That is not intended to imply we should worry about a positive accrual ratio, but it's worth noting where the accrual ratio is rather high. Notably, there is some academic evidence that suggests that a high accrual ratio is a bad sign for near-term profits, generally speaking.

Over the twelve months to September 2025, Inbound Platform recorded an accrual ratio of -0.15. That indicates that its free cash flow quite significantly exceeded its statutory profit. Indeed, in the last twelve months it reported free cash flow of JP¥316m, well over the JP¥202.0m it reported in profit. Given that Inbound Platform had negative free cash flow in the prior corresponding period, the trailing twelve month resul of JP¥316m would seem to be a step in the right direction.

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of Inbound Platform.

Our Take On Inbound Platform's Profit Performance

Inbound Platform's accrual ratio is solid, and indicates strong free cash flow, as we discussed, above. Because of this, we think Inbound Platform's earnings potential is at least as good as it seems, and maybe even better! And on top of that, its earnings per share have grown at an extremely impressive rate over the last three years. At the end of the day, it's essential to consider more than just the factors above, if you want to understand the company properly. Keep in mind, when it comes to analysing a stock it's worth noting the risks involved. When we did our research, we found 2 warning signs for Inbound Platform (1 is a bit concerning!) that we believe deserve your full attention.

Today we've zoomed in on a single data point to better understand the nature of Inbound Platform's profit. But there is always more to discover if you are capable of focussing your mind on minutiae. data.title: K&O Energy Group's (TSE:1663) Shareholders Will Receive A Bigger Dividend Than Last Year
data.description:
K&O Energy Group Inc.'s (TSE:1663) dividend will be increasing from last year's payment of the same period to ¥26.00 on 27th of March. This takes the annual payment to 1.5% of the current stock price, which unfortunately is below what the industry is paying.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

K&O Energy Group's Future Dividend Projections Appear Well Covered By Earnings

While yield is important, another factor to consider about a company's dividend is whether the current payout levels are feasible. Before making this announcement, K&O Energy Group was easily earning enough to cover the dividend. This means that most of what the business earns is being used to help it grow.

Looking forward, earnings per share could rise by 23.2% over the next year if the trend from the last few years continues. If the dividend continues along recent trends, we estimate the payout ratio will be 14%, which is in the range that makes us comfortable with the sustainability of the dividend.

Check out our latest analysis for K&O Energy Group

K&O Energy Group Has A Solid Track Record

The company has been paying a dividend for a long time, and it has been quite stable which gives us confidence in the future dividend potential. The dividend has gone from an annual total of ¥28.00 in 2015 to the most recent total annual payment of ¥52.00. This implies that the company grew its distributions at a yearly rate of about 6.4% over that duration. Dividends have grown at a reasonable rate over this period, and without any major cuts in the payment over time, we think this is an attractive combination as it provides a nice boost to shareholder returns.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

Investors could be attracted to the stock based on the quality of its payment history. It's encouraging to see that K&O Energy Group has been growing its earnings per share at 23% a year over the past five years. Earnings have been growing rapidly, and with a low payout ratio we think that the company could turn out to be a great dividend stock.

We Really Like K&O Energy Group's Dividend

Overall, we think this could be an attractive income stock, and it is only getting better by paying a higher dividend this year. Distributions are quite easily covered by earnings, which are also being converted to cash flows. Taking this all into consideration, this looks like it could be a good dividend opportunity.

data.title: FCE (TSE:9564) Is Posting Promising Earnings But The Good News Doesn’t Stop There
data.description:
FCE Inc.'s (TSE:9564) solid earnings announcement recently didn't do much to the stock price. We did some digging, and we think that investors are missing some encouraging factors in the underlying numbers.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

A Closer Look At FCE's Earnings

As finance nerds would already know, the accrual ratio from cashflow is a key measure for assessing how well a company's free cash flow (FCF) matches its profit. To get the accrual ratio we first subtract FCF from profit for a period, and then divide that number by the average operating assets for the period. You could think of the accrual ratio from cashflow as the 'non-FCF profit ratio'.

As a result, a negative accrual ratio is a positive for the company, and a positive accrual ratio is a negative. That is not intended to imply we should worry about a positive accrual ratio, but it's worth noting where the accrual ratio is rather high. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

FCE has an accrual ratio of -0.46 for the year to September 2025. Therefore, its statutory earnings were very significantly less than its free cashflow. To wit, it produced free cash flow of JP¥930m during the period, dwarfing its reported profit of JP¥662.0m. FCE's free cash flow improved over the last year, which is generally good to see.

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of FCE.

Our Take On FCE's Profit Performance

As we discussed above, FCE's accrual ratio indicates strong conversion of profit to free cash flow, which is a positive for the company. Because of this, we think FCE's underlying earnings potential is as good as, or possibly even better, than the statutory profit makes it seem! And the EPS is up 67% annually, over the last three years. At the end of the day, it's essential to consider more than just the factors above, if you want to understand the company properly. data.title: ZERO's (TSE:9028) Dividend Will Be ¥56.00
data.description:
The board of ZERO Co., Ltd. (TSE:9028) has announced that it will pay a dividend on the 16th of March, with investors receiving ¥56.00 per share. This makes the dividend yield 4.5%, which is above the industry average.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

ZERO's Future Dividend Projections Appear Well Covered By Earnings

Impressive dividend yields are good, but this doesn't matter much if the payments can't be sustained. However, prior to this announcement, ZERO's dividend was comfortably covered by both cash flow and earnings. This means that most of its earnings are being retained to grow the business.

Looking forward, earnings per share is forecast to rise by 6.2% over the next year. If the dividend continues on this path, the payout ratio could be 38% by next year, which we think can be pretty sustainable going forward.

See our latest analysis for ZERO

Dividend Volatility

The company's dividend history has been marked by instability, with at least one cut in the last 10 years. Since 2015, the annual payment back then was ¥23.00, compared to the most recent full-year payment of ¥140.30. This implies that the company grew its distributions at a yearly rate of about 20% over that duration. ZERO has grown distributions at a rapid rate despite cutting the dividend at least once in the past. Companies that cut once often cut again, so we would be cautious about buying this stock solely for the dividend income.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

With a relatively unstable dividend, it's even more important to evaluate if earnings per share is growing, which could point to a growing dividend in the future. ZERO has impressed us by growing EPS at 21% per year over the past five years. Earnings per share is growing at a solid clip, and the payout ratio is low which we think is an ideal combination in a dividend stock as the company can quite easily raise the dividend in the future.

We Really Like ZERO's Dividend

Overall, we think this could be an attractive income stock, and it is only getting better by paying a higher dividend this year. Earnings are easily covering distributions, and the company is generating plenty of cash. All of these factors considered, we think this has solid potential as a dividend stock.

Investors generally tend to favour companies with a consistent, stable dividend policy as opposed to those operating an irregular one. Still, investors need to consider a host of other factors, apart from dividend payments, when analysing a company. For instance, we've picked out 1 warning sign for ZERO that investors should take into consideration. Looking for more high-yielding dividend ideas? Try our collection of strong dividend payers.

This will take the annual payment to 1.7% of the stock price, which is above what most companies in the industry pay.

We should note that Polaris Holdings has issued stock equal to 83% of shares outstanding. Regularly doing this can be detrimental - it's hard to grow dividends per share when new shares are regularly being created.

data.title: There May Be Underlying Issues With The Quality Of Japan Business Systems' (TSE:5036) Earnings
data.description:
Investors were disappointed with Japan Business Systems, Inc.'s (TSE:5036) earnings, despite the strong profit numbers. We think that the market might be paying attention to some underlying factors that they find to be concerning.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Zooming In On Japan Business Systems' Earnings

In high finance, the key ratio used to measure how well a company converts reported profits into free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio (from cashflow). In plain english, this ratio subtracts FCF from net profit, and divides that number by the company's average operating assets over that period. You could think of the accrual ratio from cashflow as the 'non-FCF profit ratio'.

That means a negative accrual ratio is a good thing, because it shows that the company is bringing in more free cash flow than its profit would suggest. That is not intended to imply we should worry about a positive accrual ratio, but it's worth noting where the accrual ratio is rather high. Notably, there is some academic evidence that suggests that a high accrual ratio is a bad sign for near-term profits, generally speaking.

Over the twelve months to September 2025, Japan Business Systems recorded an accrual ratio of 0.21. We can therefore deduce that its free cash flow fell well short of covering its statutory profit. Even though it reported a profit of JP¥5.63b, a look at free cash flow indicates it actually burnt through JP¥3.3b in the last year. Coming off the back of negative free cash flow last year, we imagine some shareholders might wonder if its cash burn of JP¥3.3b, this year, indicates high risk.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On Japan Business Systems' Profit Performance

Japan Business Systems' accrual ratio for the last twelve months signifies cash conversion is less than ideal, which is a negative when it comes to our view of its earnings. Because of this, we think that it may be that Japan Business Systems' statutory profits are better than its underlying earnings power. But the happy news is that, while acknowledging we have to look beyond the statutory numbers, those numbers are still improving, with EPS growing at a very high rate over the last year. At the end of the day, it's essential to consider more than just the factors above, if you want to understand the company properly. With this in mind, we wouldn't consider investing in a stock unless we had a thorough understanding of the risks. When we did our research, we found 4 warning signs for Japan Business Systems (3 shouldn't be ignored!) that we believe deserve your full attention.

data.title: Mercuria Holdings (TSE:7347) Is Due To Pay A Dividend Of ¥22.00
data.description:
Mercuria Holdings Co., Ltd. (TSE:7347) has announced that it will pay a dividend of ¥22.00 per share on the 26th of March. This means that the annual payment will be 2.6% of the current stock price, which is in line with the average for the industry.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Estimates Indicate Mercuria Holdings' Could Struggle to Maintain Dividend Payments In The Future

We like to see a healthy dividend yield, but that is only helpful to us if the payment can continue. Prior to this announcement, the dividend made up 626% of earnings, and the company was generating negative free cash flows. Paying out such a large dividend compared to earnings while also not generating free cash flows is a major warning sign for the sustainability of the dividend as these levels are certainly a bit high.

Looking forward, EPS could fall by 46.1% if the company can't turn things around from the last few years. If the dividend continues along recent trends, we estimate the payout ratio could reach 1,200%, which could put the dividend in jeopardy if the company's earnings don't improve.

View our latest analysis for Mercuria Holdings

Mercuria Holdings Doesn't Have A Long Payment History

The company has maintained a consistent dividend for a few years now, but we would like to see a longer track record before relying on it. Since 2021, the dividend has gone from ¥20.00 total annually to ¥22.00. This implies that the company grew its distributions at a yearly rate of about 2.4% over that duration. It's good to see at least some dividend growth. Yet with a relatively short dividend paying history, we wouldn't want to depend on this dividend too heavily.

Dividend Growth Potential Is Shaky

The company's investors will be pleased to have been receiving dividend income for some time. However, things aren't all that rosy. Earnings per share has been sinking by 46% over the last five years. This steep decline can indicate that the business is going through a tough time, which could constrain its ability to pay a larger dividend each year in the future.

We're Not Big Fans Of Mercuria Holdings' Dividend

Overall, while some might be pleased that the dividend wasn't cut, we think this may help Mercuria Holdings make more consistent payments in the future. The company isn't making enough to be paying as much as it is, and the other factors don't look particularly promising either. Considering all of these factors, we wouldn't rely on this dividend if we wanted to live on the income.

It's important to note that companies having a consistent dividend policy will generate greater investor confidence than those having an erratic one. data.title: Sysmex’s Subsidiary Merger and Lowered Forecast Might Change the Case for Investing in TSE:6869
data.description:
• Sysmex Corporation recently announced it will absorb its wholly owned subsidiary, Sysmex Medica Co., Ltd., through an absorption-type merger effective April 1, 2026, to streamline equipment production and enhance efficiency.
  • This move comes as the company adjusts its full-year sales forecast downward due to ongoing currency pressures and challenging market conditions in China.
  • With the absorption merger aiming to improve productivity, we'll explore how these structural changes impact Sysmex's investment narrative amid international market challenges.

What Is Sysmex's Investment Narrative?

For those considering Sysmex, the big picture is about believing in its global leadership in diagnostic technology and potential for innovation, even as near-term profitability faces pressure. The recent move to absorb Sysmex Medica through a simplified merger is a tangible effort to cut complexity and raise efficiency, but because it's not expected to involve issuing shares or cash outflows, most immediate catalysts, like FDA product clearances or international expansion, likely remain unchanged. However, this reorganization does signal that Sysmex is responding directly to tough conditions in China and ongoing currency headwinds, both significant risks that prompted its recent guidance cuts and have weighed on the share price. If the merger accelerates cost savings or improves production flows, it could add stability, but current headwinds in key markets remain the dominant factor shaping near-term expectations for the business.

On the other hand, persistent pressure in China could continue impacting sales and margins. Despite retreating, Sysmex's shares might still be trading 11% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

data.title: Are Expanding Partnerships Enough to Support Klaviyo’s Value After a 33.6% Drop?

• Ever wondered whether Klaviyo is a bargain at current prices, or if there is more risk than reward hiding in the numbers? If you are eyeing this stock’s place in your portfolio, you are definitely not alone.
  • The share price has seen ups and downs lately. It slipped 3.4% this past week but gained 5.0% over the last month. These movements make investors wonder if the tides are turning or if rougher waters lie ahead after a 33.6% drop year-to-date.
  • Recent news has highlighted Klaviyo’s expanding partnerships in the e-commerce ecosystem. This move has caught the attention of both analysts and competitors. Announcements about integrations with major platforms appear to be fueling speculation about the company’s growth runway and competitive edge.
  • Klaviyo currently scores 3/6 on our valuation checks, meaning it looks undervalued on half of the key metrics our analysts use. Next, we will break down the common approaches to valuing the company, then explore a fresh perspective on what value really means for investors.

Find out why Klaviyo's -25.1% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Klaviyo Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model takes Klaviyo's expected future cash flows and calculates their present value. This gives investors a sense of what the business may actually be worth today. This method relies on long-term forecasts of free cash flow that are adjusted for time and risk.

Currently, Klaviyo's free cash flow stands at $162.6 million. Analyst estimates are available for about five years, but more distant projections are extrapolated by Simply Wall St. By 2029, projected free cash flow is expected to be around $216.1 million, with total annual free cash flows over the next decade expected to gradually increase in modest steps rather than dramatic leaps.

After applying this methodology, Klaviyo’s estimated intrinsic value comes to $11.66 per share. With the stock price much higher than this calculated value, the DCF model suggests the company is approximately 137.1% overvalued based on today’s cash flow outlook and projections.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Klaviyo may be overvalued by 137.1%. Discover 917 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Klaviyo.

Approach 2: Klaviyo Price vs Sales

The Price-to-Sales (P/S) ratio is often a preferred valuation tool for software companies like Klaviyo, especially when they are reinvesting heavily in growth and not yet reliably profitable. The P/S ratio allows us to compare a company's market value to its actual revenue, making it easier to assess its standing even if earnings fluctuate or are negative.

Not all P/S ratios are created equal. A higher P/S ratio can be justified if investors expect a company to grow revenue quickly and sustainably, or if they see less risk in future performance. On the other hand, slower growth or bigger risks tend to push that multiple down.

Klaviyo is currently trading at a P/S ratio of 7.23x. That is right in line with the average among its closest peers, which sits at 7.32x, and quite a bit above the broader software industry average of 4.43x. So, at first glance, Klaviyo appears fairly valued compared to its direct competition but at a premium to the wider sector.

Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" goes a step further by factoring in unique aspects of Klaviyo’s business, such as its revenue growth, profit margins, risk profile, industry dynamics, and market cap. This proprietary approach moves beyond simple peer or industry comparison and offers a more holistic benchmark for what Klaviyo's P/S ratio should be in an efficient market.

For Klaviyo, the Fair Ratio is calculated at 8.50x. Since the company’s actual P/S ratio is slightly below this, the analysis suggests Klaviyo may be modestly undervalued based on this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

PS ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1421 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Klaviyo Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let's introduce you to Narratives. Narratives are simply your story about a company; they connect your predictions for future revenue, profit margins, and fair value to the reasoning and context behind those estimates.

Rather than just focusing on rigid numbers, Narratives let you frame Klaviyo’s potential in your own words, linking financial forecasts with the business facts, risks, and opportunities you see. This approach helps explain not only what you think Klaviyo could be worth, but why. It gives meaning and direction to dry data.

Narratives are easily accessible to anyone in the Simply Wall St Community, where millions of investors share and update their views. You can quickly compare your fair value for Klaviyo with others, check how it stacks up against the latest price, and see real-time changes when news or earnings emerge. This means you are always working with the freshest perspectives, making it much easier to decide when to buy, hold, or sell.

For example, at the time of writing, Klaviyo's Community Narratives range from a bullish $60.00 price target based on rapid innovation and international expansion, to a more cautious $37.00 citing intense AI-led competition and margin concerns.

Do you think there's more to the story for Klaviyo? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: What Moody's (MCO)'s Raised Outlook and Efficiency Push Means For Shareholders
data.description:
• Earlier in November, Moody's Corporation reported third-quarter adjusted earnings that exceeded expectations, raised its full-year outlook for earnings, revenues, and margins, and announced a restructuring initiative aimed at operational efficiency, expected to drive meaningful cost savings through 2026.
  • A highlight from the results was growth in both Moody's Investors Service and Moody's Analytics segments, complemented by the company's new efficiency program signaling continued focus on profitability enhancements.
  • Next, we will explore how Moody's raised financial guidance and efficiency restructuring could influence its investment narrative for long-term growth.

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

Advertisement

Moody's Investment Narrative Recap

To believe in Moody’s as a shareholder, you’d need confidence in its ability to maintain leadership in credit ratings and analytics while scaling with the expanding private credit markets. The latest earnings and raised outlook reinforce the near-term catalyst of solid revenue momentum, though they do not materially lessen the ongoing risk from regulatory scrutiny and heightened compliance costs, especially as policymakers examine private credit transparency. Among recent developments, the Strategic and Operational Efficiency Restructuring Program stands out. This initiative targets substantial cost savings through 2026 and supports the ongoing margin expansion efforts, directly addressing the short-term profitability catalyst highlighted by the company’s upgraded guidance. However, it’s important to consider that persistent regulatory attention on the private credit segment could still present headwinds that investors should be aware of...

Read the full narrative on Moody's (it's free!)

Moody's is projected to achieve $9.0 billion in revenue and $3.0 billion in earnings by 2028. This outlook is based on an annual revenue growth rate of 7.3% and an earnings increase of $0.9 billion from the current $2.1 billion.

Uncover how Moody's forecasts yield a $545.50 fair value, a 14% upside to its current price.
